site_description: A community-run portal for the Municipality of Indang

hero.title: Welcome to BetterGov.ph
hero.subtitle: A community-run portal to find information and services of the Municipality of Indang, Cavite.

services.title: Services & Information
services.description: Access official government services quickly and easily. Find what you need for citizenship, business, education, and more.

governmentActivity.title: Government Activity
governmentActivity.description: Stay updated with the latest government activities and announcements.

history.title: History of Indang
history.charteredDesc: Cavite State University (CavSU), the largest state university in Cavite, has its main campus in Indang — a cornerstone of the municipality's identity as an agricultural and educational hub.
history.elevationDesc: Indang sits at 299.5 meters above sea level at coordinates 14°12'N, 120°53'E, giving it a cooler climate and fertile highlands ideal for diverse agriculture.

precolonial.title: Early Settlements
precolonial.text: The area now known as Indang was home to early Tagalog communities engaged in farming and trade. Its fertile highlands made it a natural site for permanent settlement.

spanish.title: Parish Establishment
spanish.text: Under Spanish rule, Indang was organized as a parish. The Church of Saint Gregory the Great became the spiritual and civic center of the community, drawing settlers from surrounding areas.

revolution.title: Philippine Revolution
revolution.text: Indang was an active theater during the Philippine Revolution. The Battle of Indang in 1897 was a significant engagement between Katipunan forces and Spanish troops in Cavite.

american.title: Municipal Organization
american.text: Under American colonial governance, Indang was formally organized as a municipality within the province of Cavite, establishing its local government structure.

cavsu.title: Agricultural College Founded
cavsu.text: The Indang Agricultural School was established, which would grow into Cavite State University (CavSU) — now the largest state university in Cavite with its main campus in Indang.

republic.title: Growth & Development
republic.text: After Philippine independence, Indang developed as an agricultural and educational center. Its 36 barangays across 74.90 km² grew steadily alongside the expanding CavSU community.

present.title: Modern Indang
present.text: Home to 68,699 residents (2020), Indang is a 3rd class municipality known for agriculture, education, and tourism. Facebook: @LGUIndangCavite | @TourismIndang
